problems.title: Full nonlinear stability of subextremal Kerr
problems.short_title: Nonlinear stability of subextremal Kerr (full $|a|<M$ range)
problems.summary: Prove asymptotically flat vacuum stability for the entire subextremal Kerr family; small-angular-momentum nonlinear regimes have theorem-level results, while the unrestricted subextremal range remains open.
problems.problem_statement: Prove that asymptotically flat vacuum initial data sufficiently close to a Kerr slice with $|a|<M$ evolve to a spacetime with complete future null infinity, a regular event horizon, and quantitative convergence to a nearby Kerr metric modulo gauge and parameter modulation—without restricting to $|a|\ll M$ once a full theorem is available.
problems.statement: Prove that asymptotically flat vacuum initial data sufficiently close to a Kerr slice with $|a|<M$ evolve to a spacetime with complete future null infinity, a regular event horizon, and quantitative convergence to a nearby Kerr metric modulo gauge and parameter modulation—without restricting to $|a|\ll M$ once a full theorem is available.
problems.why_it_matters: This is the central exterior vacuum stability benchmark for rotating black holes in asymptotically flat general relativity; a full theorem would anchor the nonlinear theory of Kerr as a dynamical attractor.

scope.background: Four-dimensional Einstein vacuum equations, asymptotically flat Cauchy development from a spacelike slice, comparing to the subextremal Kerr family ($|a|<M$).
scope.equation_type: Full nonlinear vacuum Einstein equations (not a fixed-background model).
scope.linearity: Nonlinear evolution; linearized theory is a tool, not the target claim.
scope.regularity: As in the cited stability program: strong enough Sobolev/decay assumptions on data to close nonlinear estimates; exact minimal regularity is part of a complete solution.
scope.parameter_regime: Target is the full subextremal range $|a|<M$ at fixed mass scale. Current theorem-level nonlinear stability is established in restricted regimes (notably small $|a|/M$), not for all subextremal parameters simultaneously.
scope.asymptotics: Asymptotically flat (no cosmological constant in the primary formulation).
scope.gauge_or_formulation: Stability is understood modulo diffeomorphisms and modulation of Kerr parameters $(M,a)$ along the flow.

known_results.statement: Nonlinear stability of Kerr for sufficiently small angular momentum per unit mass (a theorem-level result in the Klainerman–Szeftel program for $|a|/M$ small).
known_results.regime: Vacuum, asymptotically flat, $|a|/M$ small in the sense of that work.
known_results.significance: Establishes a genuine nonlinear stability theorem for Kerr in a nontrivial open subset of parameters; it does not yet cover the full subextremal family.

known_results.statement: Large body of linearized decay and mode stability results on fixed subextremal Kerr backgrounds.
known_results.regime: Linearized Einstein vacuum on exact Kerr.
known_results.significance: Necessary input and partial evidence; not equivalent to a nonlinear stability theorem for the full $|a|<M$ family.

problems.remaining_gap: Close a global nonlinear stability theorem for asymptotically flat vacuum data near arbitrary subextremal Kerr slices (all $|a|<M$), including sharp modulation of parameters, uniform trapping/superradiance estimates, and a complete causal/geometric description of the developed spacetime.
problems.status_explanation: Marked “partial” because restricted-parameter nonlinear theorems exist, while the statement as written (full subextremal family without small-$|a|$ restriction) remains open. “Partial” here does not mean the unrestricted case is nearly proved—only that some nontrivial nonlinear regimes are settled.
problems.math_required: Geometric analysis of Einstein vacuum equations; null structure equations; gauge fixing; weighted Sobolev spaces; vector-field and $r^p$ methods; trapping/superradiance control; modulation of mass and angular momentum.
problems.completion_criteria: A complete answer must specify the initial-data topology, prove global exterior existence, identify the final Kerr parameters, establish quantitative decay rates in a fixed gauge, and control nonlinear errors uniformly over the claimed subextremal parameter window.
problems.implications: Would settle the core exterior black-hole stability problem for rotating vacuum black holes and supply the exterior half of a precise strong cosmic censorship–compatible picture in the vacuum setting.
problems.fv_reason: Full nonlinear vacuum Einstein stability is a global PDE program; formalization should target lemmas and restricted regimes first.
problems.progress_summary: Nonlinear Kerr stability is proved for small $|a|/M$; the full subextremal family remains a major open frontier. Linearized decay on fixed Kerr is much better developed.

problems.title: Peeling and polyhomogeneous expansions at null infinity for nonlinear near-Kerr evolutions
problems.short_title: Peeling / polyhomogeneity at $\mathscr{I}^+$ for near-Kerr
problems.summary: Establish sharp asymptotic (peeling-type or polyhomogeneous) expansions at future null infinity for solutions of the nonlinear vacuum Einstein equations arising from near-Kerr data.
problems.problem_statement: Prove a sharp asymptotic expansion for curvature and connection coefficients at future null infinity ($\mathscr{I}^+$) for nonlinear vacuum evolutions starting from initial data sufficiently close to a Kerr slice in an asymptotically flat setting—specifying the expansion order, gauge hypotheses, leading coefficients as functionals of data, and rigorous remainder bounds appropriate to the nonlinear problem.
problems.statement: Prove a sharp asymptotic expansion for curvature and connection coefficients at future null infinity ($\mathscr{I}^+$) for nonlinear vacuum evolutions starting from initial data sufficiently close to a Kerr slice in an asymptotically flat setting—specifying the expansion order, gauge hypotheses, leading coefficients as functionals of data, and rigorous remainder bounds appropriate to the nonlinear problem.
problems.why_it_matters: Decay estimates alone do not pin down the fine radiation field; peeling and polyhomogeneous structure encode BMS charges, memory, and compatibility with scattering formulations at null infinity.

scope.background: Asymptotically flat vacuum general relativity; global nonlinear development from Cauchy data.
scope.equation_type: Nonlinear vacuum Einstein equations (full Einstein, not a linearized model on fixed Kerr).
scope.linearity: Nonlinear near-Kerr evolutions; comparison to linearized null-asymptotics is context only.
scope.regularity: Polyhomogeneous or smooth-compactified formulations as in modern null-infinity analysis.
scope.parameter_regime: Near subextremal Kerr in mass/angular momentum; the open question is the nonlinear map from data to asymptotic coefficients, not the leading linearized mode on fixed Kerr alone.
scope.asymptotics: Asymptotically flat with future null infinity in the usual sense of causal completeness toward $\mathscr{I}^+$.
scope.gauge_or_formulation: Expansion should be stated in a Bondi or conformal gauge class compatible with the nonlinear stability framework (not a purely formal expansion without remainder control).

problems.remaining_gap: Provide a theorem-level polyhomogeneous or peeling-compatible expansion with remainder estimates for solutions controlled by the nonlinear stability program (or an alternative global Cauchy theory), not only for Minkowski or linearized models.

problems.math_required: Null foliation geometry; transport equations; conformal compactification; weighted regularity along null hypersurfaces; b-analysis / polyhomogeneous calculus where applicable.
problems.completion_criteria: A complete answer must provide the expansion order, the gauge class, the leading coefficients as functionals of data, and rigorous remainder bounds uniform for the stated class of near-Kerr data.
problems.implications: Clarifies radiation memory, asymptotic charges, and compatibility between nonlinear stability and scattering/BMS-based descriptions.
problems.fv_reason: Null-asymptotic substatements often reduce to structured transport and compactified PDE—more amenable to staged formalization than full nonlinear stability.
problems.progress_summary: Polyhomogeneous asymptotics are well developed for Minkowski stability and related linearized settings; the nonlinear near-Kerr case targeted here is a distinct global PDE frontier.

problems.title: Kerr stability with BMS charges and nonlinear memory
problems.short_title: BMS charges, memory, and Kerr stability compatibility
problems.summary: Package asymptotic charges, BMS gauge choices, and nonlinear memory into a stability statement compatible with rigorous Kerr stability theory—a synthesis rather than a single classical one-line conjecture.
problems.problem_statement: Build a formulation of asymptotically flat vacuum stability compatible with Bondi–Metzner–Sachs (BMS) charge bookkeeping and nonlinear gravitational memory: define charges and memory observables in the regularity class used by the stability proof, prove their finiteness and flux laws, and show consistency with convergence to a final Kerr state modulo gauge and parameter modulation.
problems.statement: Build a formulation of asymptotically flat vacuum stability compatible with Bondi–Metzner–Sachs (BMS) charge bookkeeping and nonlinear gravitational memory: define charges and memory observables in the regularity class used by the stability proof, prove their finiteness and flux laws, and show consistency with convergence to a final Kerr state modulo gauge and parameter modulation.
problems.why_it_matters: A black-hole stability theorem should align with the asymptotic observables used in waveform and memory analysis, not only with interior decay rates.

All three must be executed at theorem level.

problems.title: Full asymptotically flat stability of the subextremal Kerr–Newman family
problems.short_title: Full Kerr–Newman stability (Einstein–Maxwell)
problems.summary: Prove linear and nonlinear stability for the full subextremal Kerr–Newman family; restricted linear results exist for weak charge and slow rotation, but the unrestricted coupled problem remains open.
problems.problem_statement: Prove linear and then nonlinear asymptotic stability of the full subextremal Kerr–Newman family ($a^2+Q^2<M^2$ in standard parameters) under coupled Einstein–Maxwell dynamics in an asymptotically flat setting—without imposing small-charge or slow-rotation restrictions once a complete theorem is available.
problems.statement: Prove linear and then nonlinear asymptotic stability of the full subextremal Kerr–Newman family ($a^2+Q^2<M^2$ in standard parameters) under coupled Einstein–Maxwell dynamics in an asymptotically flat setting—without imposing small-charge or slow-rotation restrictions once a complete theorem is available.
problems.why_it_matters: Kerr–Newman is the natural stationary charged rotating family; settling its stability is the coupled-field analogue of vacuum Kerr stability.

scope.background: Four-dimensional Einstein–Maxwell, asymptotically flat, subextremal Kerr–Newman as reference.
scope.linearity: Target includes nonlinear stability; current partial results are linearized.

scope.parameter_regime: Eventual goal is the full subextremal range $a^2+Q^2<M^2$. Existing theorem-level linear results cover restricted windows (e.g. weak charge and slow rotation).
scope.gauge_or_formulation: Lorenz / EM gauge and harmonic-type coordinates as in the linearized theory; nonlinear gauge choice is part of a complete theorem.

known_results.statement: Linearized stability (decay to a linearized Kerr–Newman solution modulo gauge) for weakly charged, slowly rotating Kerr–Newman black holes.
known_results.regime: Linearized Einstein–Maxwell, stated smallness regime of arXiv:2301.08557.
known_results.significance: Confirms partial linearized control in an open subset of parameters; does not constitute full subextremal linear or any nonlinear theorem.

problems.remaining_gap: Extend coupled linearized theory to the full subextremal parameter range and build a nonlinear modulated stability theorem comparable in scope to the vacuum Kerr program.

problems.math_required: Non-selfadjoint spectral theory; coupled gravito-EM perturbation equations; physical-space energy estimates; nonlinear modulation of mass, charge, and spin.
problems.completion_criteria: Treat the full subextremal parameter range and produce decay to linearized or nonlinear Kerr–Newman modulo gauge and parameter shifts as claimed.
problems.implications: Would give a coupled-field analogue of Kerr stability and reshape the Einstein–Maxwell scattering picture.
problems.fv_reason: Coupled Einstein–Maxwell perturbation theory; full nonlinear theorem remains large, but structured linearized subproblems exist.

problems.title: Einstein–massive Klein–Gordon near Kerr: classification of stable and unstable regimes
problems.short_title: Massive scalar on Kerr — regime classification
problems.summary: Classify long-time dynamics for massive scalar fields coupled to near-Kerr geometries; superradiant instability occurs in rigorous open parameter families, so “stability” must be regime-specific.
problems.problem_statement: For Einstein–massive Klein–Gordon with small coupling, classify dynamical outcomes near subextremal Kerr: dispersion toward Kerr, formation of long-lived quasi-bound configurations, or growth consistent with superradiant instability—stating explicit stable vs unstable mass/frequency/rotation windows at theorem level where possible, or a precise program to do so.
problems.statement: For Einstein–massive Klein–Gordon with small coupling, classify dynamical outcomes near subextremal Kerr: dispersion toward Kerr, formation of long-lived quasi-bound configurations, or growth consistent with superradiant instability—stating explicit stable vs unstable mass/frequency/rotation windows at theorem level where possible, or a precise program to do so.
problems.why_it_matters: Massive fields are the standard perturbation where pure vacuum dispersive intuition fails; superradiance can drive instability in open parameter sets.

problems.title: Strong cosmic censorship in rotating $\Lambda>0$ black-hole interiors (conditional on a spectral–interior bridge)
problems.short_title: SCC for Kerr–de Sitter type interiors (conditional)
problems.summary: Relate quasinormal-mode decay and interior extendibility for Kerr–de Sitter / Kerr–Newman–de Sitter models; conclusions depend on which SCC regularity formulation and which linear spectral input are assumed.
problems.problem_statement: Fix (i) a precise strong cosmic censorship regularity class ($C^k$ inextendibility, weaker $C^0$ formulations, etc.) and (ii) a quantitative bridge from linear spectral data (quasinormal decay on the chosen $\Lambda>0$ background) to interior Cauchy-horizon behavior. Under that pair, determine whether SCC holds or fails for rotating charged $\Lambda>0$ black holes—especially tracking how thresholds move near extremality compared with $\Lambda=0$.
problems.statement: Fix (i) a precise strong cosmic censorship regularity class ($C^k$ inextendibility, weaker $C^0$ formulations, etc.) and (ii) a quantitative bridge from linear spectral data (quasinormal decay on the chosen $\Lambda>0$ background) to interior Cauchy-horizon behavior. Under that pair, determine whether SCC holds or fails for rotating charged $\Lambda>0$ black holes—especially tracking how thresholds move near extremality compared with $\Lambda=0$.
problems.why_it_matters: Positive $\Lambda$ changes exterior decay and interior amplification; SCC statements are sensitive to both the regularity notion and the spectral input used.

scope.background: Kerr–de Sitter and Kerr–Newman–de Sitter–type solutions; interior up to Cauchy horizons.
scope.equation_type: Typically begins with test fields (scalar EM) and spectral theory; full gravity interior statements are further out.
scope.linearity: Much of the sharpest $\Lambda>0$ literature is linearized around a fixed background; nonlinear interior claims require separate hypotheses.
scope.regularity: Must name the SCC variant ($C^0$ extendibility vs higher regularity blow-up, etc.); different variants are not interchangeable.
scope.parameter_regime: Subextremal vs near-extremal rotation/charge; competition between slow QNM decay and blue-shift along inner horizons is regime-dependent.
scope.asymptotics: de Sitter / black-hole–de Sitter asymptotics as in the stated model.
scope.gauge_or_formulation: As in the cited spectral and interior PDE setups.

problems.completion_criteria: Connect spectral decay rates to extension regularity and prove either violation or restoration of SCC in a stated class matching $(A,B)$.
problems.implications: Clarifies how SCC issues differ between $\Lambda=0$ and $\Lambda>0$ rotating models.

problems.title: Hidden symmetries and approximate Carter-type operators under metric perturbation
problems.short_title: Approximate hidden symmetries near Kerr
problems.summary: Decide whether near-Kerr metrics admit controlled approximate symmetry operators usable like Carter’s constants—not the same question as photon-region spectral geometry (K-504) or Killing–Yano stability (K-508).
problems.problem_statement: Determine whether vacuum spacetimes sufficiently close to Kerr admit tensors or differential operators that (i) approximately commute with the wave operator or geodesic Hamiltonian in a quantitative sense, and (ii) reproduce enough of the Carter commutator algebra to sharpen PDE estimates—either construct them with explicit error bounds or prove a no-go theorem.
problems.statement: Determine whether vacuum spacetimes sufficiently close to Kerr admit tensors or differential operators that (i) approximately commute with the wave operator or geodesic Hamiltonian in a quantitative sense, and (ii) reproduce enough of the Carter commutator algebra to sharpen PDE estimates—either construct them with explicit error bounds or prove a no-go theorem.
problems.why_it_matters: Kerr’s integrable structure underpins many analytic tools; the open issue is whether a useful remnant survives under perturbation of the metric itself.

problems.title: Uniqueness questions for Carter-type symmetry operators on Kerr
problems.short_title: Carter operator uniqueness (scoping variants)
problems.summary: This entry intentionally lists multiple non-equivalent scoping variants for “uniqueness of the Carter symmetry operator”; maintainers must pin one before treating the problem as flagship-class.
problems.problem_statement: Clarify and then solve an appropriate uniqueness statement for Carter-type quadratic conserved quantities / symmetry operators for geodesic or scalar-wave motion on Kerr. At minimum distinguish: (a) exact operator identities on exact Kerr within a stated finite-dimensional algebra of differential operators; (b) uniqueness modulo adding functions of the obvious commuting conserved quantities; (c) robust statements for perturbed metrics where only approximate commutation holds. The database does not endorse one reading without further editorial verification.
problems.statement: Clarify and then solve an appropriate uniqueness statement for Carter-type quadratic conserved quantities / symmetry operators for geodesic or scalar-wave motion on Kerr. At minimum distinguish: (a) exact operator identities on exact Kerr within a stated finite-dimensional algebra of differential operators; (b) uniqueness modulo adding functions of the obvious commuting conserved quantities; (c) robust statements for perturbed metrics where only approximate commutation holds. The database does not endorse one reading without further editorial verification.
problems.why_it_matters: Separability on Kerr is not accidental; pinning an operator-level uniqueness theorem clarifies what survives under perturbation (cf. K-306, K-508).

problems.title: High-frequency Kerr quasinormal-mode laws with explicit remainder bounds
problems.short_title: High-frequency QNMs on Kerr — explicit remainders
problems.summary: Prove semiclassical quantization laws for QNM frequencies on fixed subextremal Kerr with explicit remainder bounds in frequency and in $(M,a)$; “explicit remainder” means stated inverse-polynomial or exponential-small error terms, not formal series alone.
problems.problem_statement: Fix a compact subextremal parameter window for $(M,a)$ and a high-frequency regime $|\omega|\to\infty$ along a chosen QNM branch. Prove an asymptotic law $\omega_n = f(n;M,a) + R_n(M,a)$ with **explicit** bounds on $R_n$ (rate in $n$, uniformity in $(M,a)$, and dependence on spin of the perturbing field). Extensions to an agreed near-Kerr class should be stated as separate theorems with modified remainders.
problems.statement: Fix a compact subextremal parameter window for $(M,a)$ and a high-frequency regime $|\omega|\to\infty$ along a chosen QNM branch. Prove an asymptotic law $\omega_n = f(n;M,a) + R_n(M,a)$ with **explicit** bounds on $R_n$ (rate in $n$, uniformity in $(M,a)$, and dependence on spin of the perturbing field). Extensions to an agreed near-Kerr class should be stated as separate theorems with modified remainders.
problems.why_it_matters: Connects rigorous spectral theory with asymptotic formulas used in ringdown interpretation.

scope.background: Linearized Teukolsky/master equations on exact Kerr (first target); near-Kerr only if hypotheses are added.

known_results.statement: Asymptotic expansions for Schwarzschild QNM frequencies with explicit leading terms (Motl’s analytic derivation).

problems.implications: Sharpens inverse-spectral programs (cf. K-507) and scattering-phase intuition.

problems.progress_summary: Physics literature and partial mathematics give asymptotic constructions; a single theorem matching the “explicit remainder” requirement here is the open packaging.

problems.title: Stability or obstruction for approximate Killing–Yano tensors near Kerr
problems.short_title: Approximate Killing–Yano fields near Kerr
problems.summary: If the Killing–Yano equation is small in an agreed tensor norm, must the metric be near Kerr, or can one build controlled counterexamples? Complements K-306 (Carter-type operators) with a 2-form/tensor PDE focus.
problems.problem_statement: Consider vacuum spacetimes with a two-form $Y_{ab}$ whose Killing–Yano equation $\nabla_{(a}Y_{b)c}=0$ is small in an agreed $L^2$ or $C^0$ norm on a suitable spacelike slice or domain. Prove either (i) a quantitative stability statement toward Kerr with explicit bounds, or (ii) an explicit family of near-vacuum solutions with small Yano violation but large distance to Kerr in a stated topology.
problems.statement: Consider vacuum spacetimes with a two-form $Y_{ab}$ whose Killing–Yano equation $\nabla_{(a}Y_{b)c}=0$ is small in an agreed $L^2$ or $C^0$ norm on a suitable spacelike slice or domain. Prove either (i) a quantitative stability statement toward Kerr with explicit bounds, or (ii) an explicit family of near-vacuum solutions with small Yano violation but large distance to Kerr in a stated topology.
problems.why_it_matters: Killing–Yano tensors encode hidden symmetry; their stability under perturbation tests whether near-Kerr geometry inherits usable algebraic structure.
